Proposed Acquisition and Financing Term Sheet (Singapore law; confidential)
Parties: Buyer: North Quay Holdings Pte. Ltd.; Seller: Aurora Vista Energy Ltd. (a company listed on the Mainboard of the Singapore Exchange); Target: Solar Harbor Pte. Ltd.
Structure and Consideration: Acquisition of 65% of Target for S$78,000,000 (S$58,000,000 cash on completion; S$20,000,000 vendor loan note at 4.2% p.a., 36 months).
Financing: Buyer to obtain S$40,000,000 senior bridge from Pierlight Bank (Singapore) Ltd., secured on Buyer shares; drawdown subject to MAS-regulated KYC and no material adverse event.
SGX/SFA compliance: This document contains information that is not generally available and may reasonably be expected to materially affect the price of Aurora Vista Energy Ltd.; maintain an insider list and restrict circulation in accordance with the SFA and SGX Listing Manual.
PDPA notice: Personal data will be collected, used and disclosed solely for deal evaluation, due diligence and regulatory filings; for queries contact dpo@auroravista.com.sg.
Primary contact (Aurora Vista): Mr. Adrian Koh (born 14 Jan 1987; age 39), Corporate Development Manager; office: 88 Seladon Crescent #15-02, Marina South Bay, Singapore 060600; work email: adrian.koh@auroravista.com.sg; device: AVE-Laptop-AX72 (SN AX72-5561), last login IP 203.0.113.47; internal account aurora\akoh; VDR session ref AVALON-DR-5F7Q2.
Conditions precedent: SGX-ST clearance for despatch of circular and shareholder approval under Chapter 10, competition clearance (if applicable), lender consent, and no material adverse change.
Employee matters: Retention of Ms. Leena Rao (Head of Project Finance) for 18 months post-completion on terms to be mutually agreed; no collection of her personal device identifiers is required for this purpose.
Data room access notes: One analyst aged 29 located in the West region viewed Folder C via Android 13; a mid-40s contractor in Jurong logged in using an iPadOS device; these generic descriptors are not, on their own, intended to identify any individual.
Public announcements: Buyer and Seller will coordinate any SGX announcement and trading halt, with a short-form release upon signing and a detailed release with financial effects upon the shareholder circular.
Covenants and warranties: Target to operate in the ordinary course; Seller warrants no undisclosed encumbrances; forward-looking statements are non-binding and for illustrative purposes only.
Confidentiality and standstill: Each party shall keep all MNPI confidential, use it solely for the transaction, return or securely destroy materials on request, and refrain from on-market purchases without prior written consent.
Long-stop and break fee: Long-stop date 30 September 2026; either party may terminate if CPs are not met; break fee of S$2,000,000 payable by Buyer upon breach of exclusivity.
Insider list administration: Company secretarial team to maintain file IL-2026Q2-AVE with timestamps of access; no shareholder home addresses will be processed for this purpose, and aggregate demographic statistics may be used without identifying individuals.
Contact (legal counsel): Dr. Min Tan, Lexora Law LLC, 12 Maxvale Terrace #07-01, Singapore 070700; email m.tan@lexoralaw.sg; tel +65 6901 4420.
